summary: A single Ginkgo integration test, 'PubSub [It] should ChannelMessage', timed out waiting to receive a message on a channel after 1.001 seconds. This caused the overall test suite for github.com/redis/go-redis/v9 to fail with 1 failure out of 1274 specs.
root_cause: The test 'PubSub [It] should ChannelMessage' at pubsub_test.go:661 timed out after 1.001s because the channel (<-chan *redis.Message, len:0, cap:10) did not receive any message within the expected window, indicating the published message was never delivered to the subscriber during this test run.

evidence.quote:
[FAILED] Timed out after 1.001s.
  Expected
      <<-chan *redis.Message | len:0, cap:10>: 0xc0012d96c0
  to receive something.
evidence.reason: Explicit Ginkgo timeout assertion failure: the channel never received a message within the 1-second deadline.
Identifies the exact failing spec name and source file.
Pinpoints the exact line in pubsub_test.go where the timeout assertion fired.
Confirms this is the sole failure that caused the overall suite to fail.
Shows the main package test run failed, triggering the make error.
Confirms exactly one test failed out of the full suite, consistent with a timing/flaky race rather than a broad regression.

suggested_fix: Investigate whether the PubSub 'should ChannelMessage' test is flaky due to a race between subscribe and publish timing. Consider increasing the receive timeout in the test at pubsub_test.go:661, adding a retry/wait loop for the subscription to be acknowledged before publishing, or running the test in isolation to determine if it fails consistently or only under load.

model_info._backfill_note: Pre-Codex-2026-05-14 F2 fix. The Claude shim now persists _model_info from every API call; this row predates that emit so resolved_model and usage are not recoverable. requested_model is set to the shim-level alias (haiku for v1, sonnet for v2) which is what the post-fix shim emits.
